content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges:
[(5, 9, 0), (2, 6, 1), (3, 4, 4), (4, 7, 4), (1, 9, 5), (0, 7, 9), (4, 8, 9), (0, 9, 14), (6, 7, 16), (7, 8, 24), (1, 4, 25), (2, 9, 27), (3, 6, 29)],

We need to find the nodes linked with node 3 exactly at time 29.

First, we will scan each edge (u, v, t) and collect the other node whenever the time t matches the queried time AND one of the nodes matches the queried node:

* Edge (5, 9, 0) has node 9 and time 0. -> Ignore.
* Edge (2, 6, 1) has node 6 and time 1. -> Add node 2.
* Edge (3, 4, 4) has node 4 and time 4. -> Ignore.
* Edge (4, 7, 4) has node 7 and time 4. -> Add node 4.
* Edge (1, 9, 5) has node 9 and time 5. -> Add node 1.
* Edge (0, 7, 9) has node 7 and time 9. -> Add node 0.
* Edge (4, 8, 9) has node 8 and time 9. -> Add node 4.
* Edge (0, 9, 14) has node 9 and time 14. -> Add node 0.
* Edge (6, 7, 16) has node 7 and time 16. -> Add node 6.
* Edge (7, 8, 24) has node 8 and time 24. -> Add node 7.
* Edge (1, 4, 25) has node 4 and time 25. -> Add node 1.
* Edge (2, 9, 27) has node 2 and time 27. -> Add node 2.
* Edge (3, 6, 29) has node 6 and time 29. -> Add node 3.

Now we have collected all the nodes that are linked with node 3 at time 29: [0, 4, 6, 7, 9].

Answer: [0, 4, 6, 7, 9]
